item.title:
MYSTERY OIL TRADES SPUR SCRUTINY BEFORE TRUMP POST
A sudden surge in oil and S&P 500 futures—$760M in trades over two minutes Monday—occurred just before Trump announced he was delaying strikes on Iran.
The early trading spike had no obvious news trigger and foreshadowed the sharp market moves after Trump’s post. Similar patterns have occurred ahead of other Trump announcements, raising questions of possible insider activity.
item.description:
MYSTERY OIL TRADES SPUR SCRUTINY BEFORE TRUMP POST

A sudden surge in oil and S&P 500 futures—$760M in trades over two minutes Monday—occurred just before Trump announced he was delaying strikes on Iran.

The early trading spike had no obvious news trigger and foreshadowed the sharp market moves after Trump’s post. Similar patterns have occurred ahead of other Trump announcements, raising questions of possible insider activity.

item.title:
SAUDI PRINCE PUSHES TRUMP TO PRESS IRAN WAR
Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman reportedly urged President Trump to continue the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, calling it a “historic opportunity” to reshape the Middle East. He argued that only toppling Iran’s hard-line government would remove the Gulf’s long-term threat.
While Trump has alternated between signaling escalation and talks of resolution, Saudi officials insist they support a peaceful outcome and are focused on defending against Iranian attacks. Analysts note Saudi and Israeli perspectives differ: a failed Iran is seen as a threat by Riyadh but a potential buffer by Jerusalem.
The ongoing conflict has already disrupted oil markets, and U.S. and Saudi officials worry about a protracted war.
item.description:
SAUDI PRINCE PUSHES TRUMP TO PRESS IRAN WAR

Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman reportedly urged President Trump to continue the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, calling it a “historic opportunity” to reshape the Middle East. He argued that only toppling Iran’s hard-line government would remove the Gulf’s long-term threat.

While Trump has alternated between signaling escalation and talks of resolution, Saudi officials insist they support a peaceful outcome and are focused on defending against Iranian attacks. Analysts note Saudi and Israeli perspectives differ: a failed Iran is seen as a threat by Riyadh but a potential buffer by Jerusalem.

The ongoing conflict has already disrupted oil markets, and U.S. and Saudi officials worry about a protracted war.

item.title: MORE DOLLARS HAVE CIRCULATED IN VENEZUELA'S ECONOMY IN LAST TWO MONTHS THAN IN WHOLE OF 2025, ASSISTANT SECRETARY OF US STATE DEPARTMENT SAYS

item.title:
TURKEY WEIGHS GOLD DEFENSE AS WAR SHAKES MARKETS
The Iran war is driving sharp regional volatility, with Turkey preparing measures to protect its currency.
Officials say the central bank is considering using its gold reserves, including possible gold-for-FX swaps in London.
The move highlights growing pressure on emerging markets as conflict risks escalate.
item.description:
TURKEY WEIGHS GOLD DEFENSE AS WAR SHAKES MARKETS

The Iran war is driving sharp regional volatility, with Turkey preparing measures to protect its currency.

Officials say the central bank is considering using its gold reserves, including possible gold-for-FX swaps in London.

The move highlights growing pressure on emerging markets as conflict risks escalate.

item.description: IRAN SAYS IT FIRED NEW MISSILE WAVE AT ISRAEL: NOUR NEWS

item.title:
MUSK PLANS AI CHIP MEGA-FACTORY
Elon Musk says Tesla and SpaceX will build a major chip facility in Austin to meet soaring AI demand.
The “Terafab” site will include two fabs—one for Tesla’s AI (cars, robots) and one for SpaceX’s space-based data centers. Musk says current suppliers can’t meet future needs.
He aims for the plant to produce 1 terawatt of computing power annually—about double current U.S. capacity.
No timeline was given, and the project faces execution risk, but it highlights growing pressure for in-house chip production.
item.description:
MUSK PLANS AI CHIP MEGA-FACTORY

Elon Musk says Tesla and SpaceX will build a major chip facility in Austin to meet soaring AI demand.

The “Terafab” site will include two fabs—one for Tesla’s AI (cars, robots) and one for SpaceX’s space-based data centers. Musk says current suppliers can’t meet future needs.

He aims for the plant to produce 1 terawatt of computing power annually—about double current U.S. capacity.

No timeline was given, and the project faces execution risk, but it highlights growing pressure for in-house chip production.

item.title: MUSK: TESLA AND SPACEX TO JOINTLY BUILD ADVANCED CHIP MANUFACTURING PLANT
item.description: MUSK: TESLA AND SPACEX TO JOINTLY BUILD ADVANCED CHIP MANUFACTURING PLANT

item.title:
KALSHI TEAMS WITH FIS TO BOOST PREDICTION MARKETS
Kalshi is partnering with FIS to build clearing infrastructure aimed at accelerating institutional adoption of prediction markets.
The deal integrates prediction trading into FIS systems, lowering barriers for large firms and enabling real-time clearing and higher-volume trades.
Kalshi, which saw $10.4B in monthly volume, says stronger “post-trade” infrastructure is key to the next growth phase.
The move signals a shift beyond retail users, positioning prediction markets for broader Wall Street participation.
item.description:
KALSHI TEAMS WITH FIS TO BOOST PREDICTION MARKETS

Kalshi is partnering with FIS to build clearing infrastructure aimed at accelerating institutional adoption of prediction markets.

The deal integrates prediction trading into FIS systems, lowering barriers for large firms and enabling real-time clearing and higher-volume trades.

Kalshi, which saw $10.4B in monthly volume, says stronger “post-trade” infrastructure is key to the next growth phase.

The move signals a shift beyond retail users, positioning prediction markets for broader Wall Street participation.

item.title: THAI SHIP PASSED THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ, IRAN'S TASNIM SAYS
item.description: THAI SHIP PASSED THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ, IRAN'S TASNIM SAYS

item.title:
IRAN HALTS GAS TO TURKEY AFTER STRIKE
Iran has stopped gas exports to Turkey following an Israeli strike on the South Pars field.
Turkey relied on Iran for about 14% of its gas but continues to receive supplies from Russia and Azerbaijan, with storage reserves available. The duration of the halt is unclear.
The strike—and Iran’s retaliation on regional energy sites—has pushed European gas prices sharply higher, still around 66% above pre-war levels.
South Pars is critical to Iran’s energy system, and the disruption highlights growing risks to regional supply.
item.description:
IRAN HALTS GAS TO TURKEY AFTER STRIKE

Iran has stopped gas exports to Turkey following an Israeli strike on the South Pars field.

Turkey relied on Iran for about 14% of its gas but continues to receive supplies from Russia and Azerbaijan, with storage reserves available. The duration of the halt is unclear.

The strike—and Iran’s retaliation on regional energy sites—has pushed European gas prices sharply higher, still around 66% above pre-war levels.

South Pars is critical to Iran’s energy system, and the disruption highlights growing risks to regional supply.

item.title:
CHINA’S $1.6 TRILLION FUND REKINDLES TIES WITH US MONEY MANAGERS
China’s $1.57 trillion sovereign wealth fund - long one of the biggest backers of private equity firms in the world - is considering new allocations to US money managers just months after reducing its exposure to the world’s biggest economy.
item.description:
CHINA’S $1.6 TRILLION FUND REKINDLES TIES WITH US MONEY MANAGERS

China’s $1.57 trillion sovereign wealth fund - long one of the biggest backers of private equity firms in the world - is considering new allocations to US money managers just months after reducing its exposure to the world’s biggest economy.

item.title:
BITCOIN BOTTOM IN, $MSTR HOLDS STRONG
Bernstein says Bitcoin has likely bottomed and is set to rise, keeping a $150K price target for 2026.
Despite a 50% drop from its peak, Strategy (MSTR) has remained resilient, now holding 3.6% of total Bitcoin supply (~$53.5B). The firm sees MSTR as a high-beta play on Bitcoin, backed by a strong balance sheet.
Instead of cutting exposure, Strategy added more Bitcoin at recent lows, raising $7.3B in 2026 to expand its holdings.
item.description:
BITCOIN BOTTOM IN, $MSTR HOLDS STRONG

Bernstein says Bitcoin has likely bottomed and is set to rise, keeping a $150K price target for 2026.

Despite a 50% drop from its peak, Strategy (MSTR) has remained resilient, now holding 3.6% of total Bitcoin supply (~$53.5B). The firm sees MSTR as a high-beta play on Bitcoin, backed by a strong balance sheet.

Instead of cutting exposure, Strategy added more Bitcoin at recent lows, raising $7.3B in 2026 to expand its holdings.

item.title:
AGING POWER GRIDS POSE SECURITY RISK
JPMorgan warns outdated electricity grids are now a “national security risk,” vulnerable to extreme weather, cyberattacks, and rising energy shocks.
Surging demand from AI, electrification, and industry—combined with geopolitical instability—is straining already fragile systems, creating bottlenecks and supply risks.
The bank sees a “massive” investment opportunity in grid upgrades, with global spending expected to reach $5.8 trillion by 2035, including about $1 trillion in the U.S.
Grids are shifting from neglected infrastructure to strategic assets, as resilience and energy independence become top priorities.
item.description:
AGING POWER GRIDS POSE SECURITY RISK

JPMorgan warns outdated electricity grids are now a “national security risk,” vulnerable to extreme weather, cyberattacks, and rising energy shocks.

Surging demand from AI, electrification, and industry—combined with geopolitical instability—is straining already fragile systems, creating bottlenecks and supply risks.

The bank sees a “massive” investment opportunity in grid upgrades, with global spending expected to reach $5.8 trillion by 2035, including about $1 trillion in the U.S.

Grids are shifting from neglected infrastructure to strategic assets, as resilience and energy independence become top priorities.

item.title: *IRAN-TURKEY GAS FLOW SAID TO STOP AFTER SOUTH PARS STRIKE
item.description: *IRAN-TURKEY GAS FLOW SAID TO STOP AFTER SOUTH PARS STRIKE

item.title:
OIL SHOCK FUELS INFLATION RISK
Goldman Sachs warns the Iran conflict could reignite U.S. inflation via rising oil prices.
Brent is seen at $105 in March and $115 in April if supply is disrupted, with worst-case scenarios nearing record highs. A 10% oil increase could add 0.2 points to headline inflation.
Higher fertilizer and metal costs may also lift food prices ~1.5%.
Goldman now expects 3.1% inflation by end-2026 and sees a 30% recession risk, with rate cuts still likely but less certain if oil spikes further.
item.description:
OIL SHOCK FUELS INFLATION RISK

Goldman Sachs warns the Iran conflict could reignite U.S. inflation via rising oil prices.

Brent is seen at $105 in March and $115 in April if supply is disrupted, with worst-case scenarios nearing record highs. A 10% oil increase could add 0.2 points to headline inflation.

Higher fertilizer and metal costs may also lift food prices ~1.5%.

Goldman now expects 3.1% inflation by end-2026 and sees a 30% recession risk, with rate cuts still likely but less certain if oil spikes further.

SECURITIZE TO BECOME FIRST DIGITAL TRANSFER AGENT ON NEW NYSE PLATFORM

item.title:
GULF STATES MOVE TOWARD IRAN WAR
Saudi Arabia and the UAE are reportedly edging closer to the Iran conflict. Riyadh has opened a key air base to the US, while the UAE shut Iranian-linked sites.
Missiles may have been launched from Bahrain, though US involvement with regional partners is unclear.
Oil rose and stocks fell on the news, reflecting fears of escalation as Iran continues retaliatory strikes across the region.
item.description:
GULF STATES MOVE TOWARD IRAN WAR

Saudi Arabia and the UAE are reportedly edging closer to the Iran conflict. Riyadh has opened a key air base to the US, while the UAE shut Iranian-linked sites.

Missiles may have been launched from Bahrain, though US involvement with regional partners is unclear.

Oil rose and stocks fell on the news, reflecting fears of escalation as Iran continues retaliatory strikes across the region.
